title: Sports Network Portal — Mobile App Demo · Players, Coaches & Parents

scenes.title: SNP Mobile — the app that ties a club together
scenes.narration: this is the snp mobile app. one install, one login, and a whole club fits in your pocket. players check their training load, coaches mark attendance pitch-side, parents apply to academies, all from the same shell. let's walk through it persona by persona, screen by screen.

scenes.title: Splash · SNP boot
scenes.narration: tap the icon and the splash kicks in. behind the scenes the app restores your session, fetches your role from the backend, and preloads the right language bundle. by the time the wordmark fades, the home screen already knows who you are.

scenes.title: Intro reels · routing to role-aware home
scenes.narration: first-time users see three short reels — train smarter, coach better, follow your club. swipe through or hit skip. once you land on get started, the router checks your role and drops you straight onto the right home screen, no extra menus to dig through.

scenes.title: Phone OTP · sign in
scenes.narration: auth is phone-first. pick your country code, type the number, tap send otp. a six-digit code lands by sms, you key it in, and you're in. on the next launch the app offers face id or fingerprint, so families on shared devices can still keep accounts separate.

scenes.title: Language picker + parent-of-child link
rows: register as parent · link child profile
scenes.narration: next, language. english, العربية, or français — and arabic flips the entire layout right-to-left, including charts and menus. on signup there's a register as parent toggle. flip it on and you can attach a child profile, which is how a parent gets to see junior's attendance and reports later on.

scenes.title: Player home · attendance widget
scenes.narration: now meet the player. yousef opens the app and sees three status cards up top — training load high, readiness eight out of ten, latest test results. just below, the attendance widget shows eighteen present days, two absent, on a monthly calendar he can scroll. if he trains with multiple groups, he flips between them from the same card.

scenes.title: Performance stats · AI insight
rows: ai insight · recovery dip on tuesdays
scenes.narration: tap view full stats and the performance module opens up. a twelve-week progress chart, overall rating seven point eight, five sessions this week, sprint times trending down. an ai insight banner flags a recovery dip on tuesdays — same engine that powers the coach dashboard, just summarized for the athlete.

scenes.title: Tournament card · riyadh spring cup 2026
rows: Riyadh Spring Cup 2026 · King Fahd | كأس الربيع
scenes.narration: scroll a little further and you hit the tournament card. riyadh spring cup twenty twenty-six at king fahd stadium, his u-sixteen match is on the sixteenth of march at six pm. he saves it to his schedule, gets a match-day reminder push the morning of, and the venue, kit color, and meeting time all sit on the card.

scenes.title: My groups · chat preview
scenes.narration: my groups gives him a chat thread per training group. coach ahmed can drop a pdf training plan, share a clip from yesterday's drills, or just remind everyone to bring shin guards. unread badges roll up to the home screen so nothing slips past.

scenes.title: Profile · edit info, language, leave club
scenes.narration: from the side menu he opens his profile. update the photo, change password, tune which notifications he wants — match reminders yes, marketing no. and if he ever moves on, leave club is right here, gated behind a confirm dialog so it doesn't fire by accident.

scenes.title: Coach home · today's sessions
scenes.narration: switch personas — meet coach ahmed. his home screen leads with today's three sessions, pitches and times. below that, an academy programs card for the syllabus he runs, and a tournament block with two quick actions, new request and view all. if he's also on payroll, an employee mode button up top jumps him into staff features.

scenes.title: My groups · roster + training plan
scenes.narration: from the groups tab he sees every squad he runs. tap u-sixteen football a and four pills appear — attendance, training plan, monitoring, reports. each player drills down into stats, attendance history, clinic visits, and coach feedback. it's the same data the player sees, just with edit rights.

scenes.title: Attendance marking · pitch-side
scenes.narration: this is where the app earns its keep. on the touchline he opens today's attendance, taps each name — present, late, absent — adds a reason if needed. mark all present handles the easy days. as soon as he hits save, every player and every linked parent gets the update in real time.

scenes.title: Group chat · players + parents
rows: Parent of Yousef: will be 10 min late
scenes.narration: the group chat is one shared thread for players and parents. the coach pins this week's plan as a pdf, drops a video drill, and one of the parents replies that yousef will be ten minutes late. no more whatsapp groups going stale — it all lives inside the club app.

scenes.title: Tournament request · new submission
scenes.narration: when the coach wants to enter a new tournament, he taps new request. a multi-step form collects name, sport, dates, budget, venue, and the player roster from his groups. on submit it lands in the admin's web portal for approval, and he can track status right from this card.

scenes.title: Guest home · menu cards
rows: VIP Club Member | عضو نادي كبار الشخصيات
scenes.narration: now switch to fatima, a parent who just installed the app. her home is a clean four-tile menu — club, school, academy program, vip club member. she hasn't joined anything yet, so the app keeps it simple. a little clipboard icon up top tracks any applications she opens.

scenes.title: Club profile · follow
rows: Riyadh Falcons Sports Club | نادي صقور الرياض
scenes.narration: she taps club, searches riyadh falcons, and lands on their profile. bio, sports offered, photo gallery, upcoming events including an open day on the twenty-second. she hits follow, and from now on the club's announcements push straight to her phone.

scenes.title: Academy application · 4 steps
scenes.narration: she picks academy program. step one captures her child yousef's details — name, date of birth, government id. step two collects sport, age group, gender, and physical stats like height and weight. step three uploads birth certificate and medical clearance. step four is the review and final submit. once she submits, the application is on its way to the coach.

scenes.title: Application status · my applications
rows: Riyadh Falcons · Football U-16 — under review · 2d ago
Jeddah Eagles · Swimming U-12 — additional info needed
scenes.narration: the clipboard icon opens my applications. she sees three — falcons under review, eagles asking for extra info, and tabuk already approved. when a coach needs more documents, she taps in, adds them, and resubmits without starting over.

scenes.title: VIP club services + entry passes
bullets: qr entry pass at the gate
scenes.narration: vip club is the premium tier for non-players — parents, fans, casual members. she subscribes to premium gym access and a family pool pass. each subscription generates a qr entry pass she scans at the gate, plus access to members-only events like the fourteenth of june members night.

scenes.title: Club search · follow multiple clubs
scenes.narration: search isn't limited to one club. she filters by country and sport, follows three more academies, and her feed now pulls events and announcements from every club she follows. one app, many clubs — useful when siblings train in different places.

scenes.title: Push notifications · deep-linked
rows: Coach Ahmed marked Yousef present · 4:02 PM
New message in U-16 Football A
scenes.narration: every event in the system can fire a push — attendance marked, application approved, tournament reminder, new chat message. each one deep-links straight to the relevant screen, so a tap on the application notification opens that exact application, not a generic inbox.

scenes.title: Multi-profile switcher · parent ↔ child
scenes.narration: tap her name at the top and the profile switcher slides in. fatima the parent, yousef the player, layla the younger sister. one tap and the entire app re-skins — different home screen, different menu, different language even. kids see an age-appropriate view, parents see the oversight view.

scenes.title: Applicant submits · falcons U-16
scenes.narration: let's zoom into admissions end-to-end. fatima submits yousef's application to falcons u-sixteen. crucially, the form captures his previous club — eagles u-fourteen last season. that one field unlocks the cross-club history coaches care about.

scenes.title: Coach review · cross-club history
scenes.narration: over on coach ahmed's phone, the application drops into pending admissions. because yousef came from another snp club, the coach sees the full history — ninety-two percent attendance last season, seven point six rating, even a note from his previous coach about his work rate. no more blind tryouts.

scenes.title: Approved · joined the club
scenes.narration: ahmed taps approve. instantly, yousef is auto-assigned to u-sixteen football a, his first session lands on the calendar, and both yousef and fatima get a welcome push. yousef's external user home upgrades to the full player home we saw earlier — performance, attendance, group chat, all unlocked.

scenes.title: Arabic · French · English on the fly
scenes.narration: language is a first-class feature, not a setting buried six menus deep. from profile, pick english, العربية, or français and the entire app re-renders without a restart. arabic mirrors the layout, flips the charts, and even pulls arabic versions of the club's content where available.

scenes.title: your club, in everyone's pocket
scenes.narration: that's the snp mobile app — one shell, one login, four very different experiences for players, coaches, parents, and fans. attendance, performance, admissions, tournaments, chat, vip access, all wired to the same backend that powers the club's web portal. your club, in everyone's pocket.
